regions.us-ports: Today I am monitoring Miami where current conditions are NW 2kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability and thunderstorms are forecast for Day 1 with SSE 9kt that I am also tracking for nearby Port Everglades. With Miami at NW 2kt and partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, I expect embarkation and disembarkation to proceed under routine procedures while I watch the SSE 9kt thunderstorm forecast for lightning and sudden wind shifts affecting shore excursion timing. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Miami is forecast SSE 8kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability for Day 2, and I am briefing Port Canaveral and Tampa Bay to prepare for the SSE 8kt wind trend during gangway and tender checks. Beyond 48 hours Miami trends to SSE 10kt with thunderstorms and 11% rain probability on Day 3, and I will continue tracking those SSE 10kt winds plus visibility and sea state changes before issuing any operational notices to passengers and crew.
